title: carex axillaris subsp. boenninghausiana
intro: Carex axillaris subsp. boenninghausiana, also known as Boenninghausen's sedge, is a type of flowering plant native to the subtropical regions of Eastern Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Carex axillaris subsp. boenninghausiana include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
